proposed_relations.description: The winter 2012 Hylan Boulevard newsletter documents the S79 SBS as the streamlined replacement for the existing S79 local service. The newsletter headline (p001_c0003) announces 'S79 SELECT BUS SERVICE IMPROVEMENTS' and describes the S79 SBS rerouting at Staten Island Mall (p003_c0009, p003_c0012) while item 1 (p001_c0005) notes the existing S79 would be streamlined with fewer stops.

evidence_refs.source_quote: S79 SELECT BUS SERVICE IMPROVEMENTS
1. Streamlined S79 bus service would operate with fewer stops and a more direct route to save travel time.
Streamlined Bus Service (Rerouting of Staten Island Mall-bound S79)
▲ S79 SBS stays on Staten Island Mall's Ring Road

Per rule, own base_record_id match means 'new' — observation already canonically resolves.

proposed_relations.description: The Hylan Boulevard Transportation Improvement Study encompasses S79 Select Bus Service improvements as one of its key components. The newsletter explicitly lists S79 SBS improvements — including streamlined service, fewer stops, bus lanes, TSP, and pedestrian safety enhancements — as part of the broader corridor study.

evidence_refs.source_quote: S79 SELECT BUS SERVICE IMPROVEMENTS

1. Streamlined S79 bus service would operate with fewer stops and a more direct route to save travel time. 2. Optimized signal timing improves progression of traffic. 3. Transit Signal Priority (TSP) extends the green light or shortens the red light to keep buses moving. 4. Bus lanes at key locations bring faster and more reliable service to all bus riders.

rationale: The observation's base_record_id (project_hylan-blvd-transportation-improvement-study) matches the existing registry record exactly. The registry record already includes source_id 2012_sbs_hylan_winter_newsletter, and the observation captures the same project entity — the Hylan Boulevard Transportation Improvement Study — as documented in the same newsletter source. Per canonicalization rules, when the closest match is the observation's own base_record_id, the decision is new (the observation already resolves to that id). The observation adds a more specific stage status ('Present Final Plan, Winter/Spring 2012') consistent with the project timeline shown in the newsletter, but does not constitute a distinct project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Hylan Boulevard Transportation Improvement Study
Present Final Plan (Winter/Spring 2012)

rationale: The observation's base_record_id 'corridor_hylan-boulevard-brt' already matches the canonical registry record for Hylan Boulevard BRT corridor on Staten Island. The record's source_ids already include '2012_sbs_hylan_winter_newsletter', the payload matches (corridor_name 'Hylan Boulevard', borough 'Staten Island'), and no other registry record better represents this observation. Since the base_record_id is the closest match and no different record should be linked, the decision is 'new' — the observation is already correctly resolved.
